summary.intent: Test and debug the new session spawning workflow in the Gossamer Electron app, specifically investigating delays in the SessionDetail conversation UI not reflecting xterm terminal events promptly.
summary.outcome: Identified that the 2000ms server-side DB polling interval was the root cause of WS broadcast latency. Reduced server poller to 500ms and added a 500ms client-side polling fallback in SessionDetail for live sessions.

repo: Session updates flow via a polling chain: hook writes to SQLite → server poller detects DB change → broadcasts sessions_updated WS → SessionDetail fetches log events. The WS is a delivery mechanism for a polled signal, not a push-from-source stream.
SpawnSessionScreen matches a newly spawned session by filtering /api/sessions for entries with startedAt >= spawnedAt - 5000ms, creating a narrow 5-second detection window.
The server uses node-pty behind a WebSocket for terminal emulation; xterm.js on the frontend connects to this WS and runs the claude command 300ms after connect.

code.finding: handleSpawn escapes single quotes in the prompt and calls openSpawnTab(cwd, "claude '<prompt>'", title)
New session button opens a modal with prompt textarea and optional working directory selector populated from repoStatuses
openSpawnTab creates a spawn tab with a spawnedAt timestamp used downstream for session matching
Terminal WebSocket handler spawns a PTY shell in the given cwd and forwards data bidirectionally
Server broadcast poller was set to 2000ms; reduced to 500ms to cut WS notification lag for all clients

workflow: Build + type-check (tsc) were run as a quick sanity check after reading code before manual testing — both passed cleanly before the bug was reported.
Reducing a server-side polling interval is a broader fix than client-side polling; client-side polling serves as a belt-and-suspenders fallback for missed WS messages.

friction: The WS appeared to be working (subscription was receiving messages) but the actual latency source was the server-side DB poller, not the WS transport — this required tracing the full event chain to diagnose.
The 5-second spawnedAt matching window in SpawnSessionScreen may miss sessions if the hook handler is slow to write to the DB — identified as a potential fragility but not yet addressed.
open_items: The 5-second session detection window in SpawnSessionScreen (spawnedAt - 5000ms) could miss sessions under slow DB write conditions — may need a more robust matching strategy.
The client-side 500ms poll in SessionDetail for isLive sessions is a fallback, not a primary fix — consider whether the server should push events more directly rather than relying on polling at all.

Several files were modified (Shell.tsx, TabsContext.tsx, EmbeddedTerminal.tsx, SessionRow.tsx, ActiveSessions.tsx, LauncherScreen.tsx) as part of the broader new session workflow — these were not all reviewed in the transcript and may contain related tech debt.
